answer: Rope365 says to take time and care and not pull the wrist beyond its range limit because forcing it too far can cause injury.
evidence: Day 180: Wrist Lock – Pulling the hand back into a joint lock is a very restrictive position. Explore how this principle can be used in a tie by tying the palm or finger and pulling the wrist backward. Take time and care as pulling the wrist against its limit can lead to an injury if we push too far.
expected_answer: Take time and care, and do not pull the wrist beyond its range limit, because forcing it too far can cause injury.

answer: Rope365 recommends warming up each joint, especially the shoulders, and adapting the position to the person's flexibility.
evidence: Bringing the hands in the back adds an element of vulnerability and helplessness. These positions challenge the body in new ways as they are not often used in daily life. Warm-up each joint, especially the shoulder to allow for maximum range of motion and prevent injuries. Every person has a different flexibility level and these poses will look different from person to person.
expected_answer: Warm up each joint, especially the shoulders, and adapt the position to the person's flexibility.
